evals.name: single-agent-implementation-summary
evals.prompt: I just finished implementing the changes for task-101. The work added a paginated admin user listing endpoint at GET /api/admin/users (replacing a previous draft that had verbs in the URL and SQL injection issues — fixed both). I wrote 8 unit tests, all passing. typecheck and lint are clean. The endpoint requires admin permission via a require_admin dependency. Write the implementation summary I should hand to the reviewer before I commit. The work was single-agent, full process, on branch feature/task-101-admin-users targeting develop. No migrations, no config changes. Files changed: api/routes/admin.py, api/auth.py (added require_admin), api/schemas/user_out.py (new DTO), tests/api/test_admin_users.py.
expectations: Output uses the implementation summary format (not the worker handoff or closeout format)
All 7 fields appear in order: Process Used, Execution Context, Task Scope, Implementation Outcome, Behavioral Impact, Risks/Gaps, File Reference Summary, Governance Status
Process Used: Full process
Execution Context: Single-agent
Task Scope names task-101 explicitly
Implementation Outcome includes the active branch (feature/task-101-admin-users) and the integration target (develop)
Quality gate results are concrete (8 unit tests passing, typecheck clean, lint clean) — not vague claims like 'all gates passed'
Governance Status indicates the work is awaiting code review (pre-commit; commit/push haven't happened yet)
File Reference Summary names the 4 files changed without dumping a full changelog
Output does not cite this skill by name (no 'task-handoff-summaries', 'this skill', 'as required')
Output does not falsely claim commit, push, or merge happened (those steps haven't run yet)

evals.name: multi-agent-worker-handoff
evals.prompt: I'm a multi-agent worker. I finished my slice of bead proj-42-w2 (staging-branch source-ref enforcement workflow). Branch is feature/proj-42-w2_staging-source-ref. The branch is published to remote. Commit SHA at handoff: 7a3f9b1. Quality gates: lint passed (0 errors), typecheck passed, unit tests 142/142 passing. The intended integration target is the parent integration branch integration/proj-42_staging-bringup. One area to watch in integration: the source-ref check job uses `${{ github.head_ref }}` which works for PR events but not for direct branch pushes — the parent epic doesn't allow direct pushes to staging/main so this should be fine, but the orchestrator should confirm. No known issues otherwise. Write the worker handoff summary for the orchestrator.
expectations: Output uses the worker handoff summary format (not the implementation or closeout format)
All 6 fields appear: Task (or Bead), Branch with Published+Commit, Implementation Outcome with integration target, Quality Gates, Risks/Gaps, Review Notes
Task identifier names proj-42-w2 explicitly
Branch field names feature/proj-42-w2_staging-source-ref, marks Published as yes, includes commit SHA 7a3f9b1
Quality Gates section reports concrete results (142/142 unit tests, 0 lint errors, typecheck passed) — not vague
Intended integration target is named (integration/proj-42_staging-bringup)
Review Notes call out the github.head_ref consideration the worker wants the orchestrator to verify
Output is concise (worker handoff is shorter than the user-facing implementation summary; no Behavioral Impact / File Reference Summary unless explicitly relevant)
Output does not falsely claim the branch was accepted, merged, or that closeout happened — handoff is pre-acceptance
Output does not cite this skill by name

evals.name: closeout-summary-with-evidence
evals.prompt: Closeout time. Bead proj-42 (Add staging branch and 3-tier deploy flow) is done. Single-agent work using the full bead process. Active branch was feature/proj-42_staging-branch in the example repo. Integration target reached: develop (merged successfully). Quality gate results: lint 0 errors, typecheck 0 errors, unit tests 142/142 passing, build succeeded, e2e tests deferred (no CI secrets at adoption — documented in §13 of the policy doc). UAT was not requested for this work (infrastructure-only change, no user-facing behavior). Commit SHA: 9a2b3c4. Pushed: yes. Branch cleanup: merged to develop, local feature/proj-42_staging-branch deleted, remote deleted. Behavioral impact: develops new branch hierarchy (main/staging/develop), introduces source-ref enforcement, adds CI workflows for ci-develop/ci-staging/ci-main, adds simple-git-hooks pre-commit lint. Files: docs/development/staging-branch.md (new), docs/development/branches.md (amended §1, §8), docs/development/commits.md (amended UAT Gate section), docs/development/testing.md (amended), .github/workflows/ci-develop.yml (new), .github/workflows/ci-staging.yml (new), .github/workflows/ci-main.yml (new), .nvmrc (new), package.json (added simple-git-hooks). Risks: GitHub branch protection / rulesets are NOT applied (the GitHub plan tier in use doesn't support rulesets on private repos) — policies binding by review discipline only until plan upgrade. e2e/integration test secrets not provisioned, those gates won't pass on day one. Lint/typecheck/unit baseline at adoption: 200 lint errors, 80 typecheck errors, 12 failing unit tests on develop tip — making those required gates would freeze the repo. Promotion to develop reached; no PR open to uat or main yet. Write the closeout summary.
expectations: Output uses the closeout summary format (not implementation or worker handoff)
All 7 sections appear: Process Used, Execution Context, Task Scope, Closeout Outcome, Evidence, Behavioral Impact, Risks/Gaps, File Reference Summary
Process Used: Full process (or Full bead process)
Execution Context: Single-agent
Task Scope names proj-42 with the bead title
Closeout Outcome names the active branch and the integration target reached (develop)
Evidence section is concrete: lint/typecheck/unit/build with numbers, e2e deferred with reason, UAT status, commit SHA 9a2b3c4, push yes, branch cleanup status, promotion stopped at develop

Risks/Gaps captures the concrete unresolved items: branch protection not applied (with reason), e2e/integration secrets not provisioned, baseline error counts on develop, promotion not yet to uat or main
File Reference Summary names the relevant files without dumping a full changelog (docs amendments and new workflow files)
Output does not falsely claim the work reached uat or main when it stopped at develop
Output does not cite this skill by name

evals.name: lightweight-no-task-closeout
evals.prompt: Quick one — I just merged a 1-line typo fix (changed 'pubish' to 'publish' in a button label in our admin UI). No bead. No PRD. Single-agent. Branch fix/typo-publish-button merged to main directly (allowed for trivial typo fixes per team convention). Commit SHA: 4d5e6f7. Pushed. Branch deleted. lint passed, typecheck passed, unit tests still pass (no test impact). Write a closeout summary.
expectations: Output uses the closeout summary format
Process Used: Lightweight process
Execution Context: Single-agent
Task Scope explicitly states 'No task was worked on.' (or equivalent — 'No bead was worked on.')
Output does NOT invent a task ID, ticket number, or fictional bead reference
Closeout Outcome describes the typo fix and that it merged directly to main
Evidence section is concrete (lint passed, typecheck passed, unit tests passing, commit 4d5e6f7, pushed, branch deleted)
Behavioral Impact notes the user-visible button label change (pubish → publish)
Risks/Gaps states there are no known remaining gaps (or equivalent)
Output does not cite this skill by name
